steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says that while most countries saw an increase in women judges, some countries saw the number decrease or remain unchanged. The blank needs specific country examples that show one or both of those patterns between 2009 and 2013.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — the number in Slovenia was greater in 2013 than in 2009, whereas the number in the Dominican Republic was lower in 2013 than in 2009.: Slovenia increased from 4 to 6, which contradicts the blank's requirement. ✗
  • Choice B — the number in Slovenia was greater in 2013 than in 2009, whereas the number in Finland was the same in 2013 as in 2009.: Slovenia increased, so this choice includes a country that doesn't fit. ✗
  • Choice C — the number in Finland was the same in 2013 as in 2009, but it was greater than the numbers in Slovenia and the Dominican Republic in 2013.: This includes an unchanged country but not a decreased one, so it does not fit the decreased-or-unchanged contrast as well as D. ✗
  • Choice D — the number in the Dominican Republic was lower in 2013 than in 2009, whereas the number in Finland was the same in 2013 as in 2009.: The Dominican Republic dropped from 5 to 4 (decreased) and Finland held steady at 7 (unchanged) — both fit the required patterns. ✓
That is the correct answer.
